title: Chinese scientists achieve major breakthrough in scalable quantum networks
why:
- A research team from the University of Science and Technology of China achieved significant advances in scalable quantum networks, bringing the technology closer to real-world application.
- The team developed a long-lived trapped-ion quantum memory, a highly efficient ion-photon interface, and a high-fidelity experimental protocol, enabling quantum entanglement that persists long enough for practical network use.
- This is the world's first demonstration of a scalable building block for a quantum repeater, critical for long-distance quantum networks.
- The team achieved high-fidelity entanglement between two distant rubidium atoms and demonstrated device-independent quantum key distribution (DI-QKD) over city-scale fiber networks for the first time.
- They implemented DI-QKD over 11 kilometers of fiber, increasing the attainable distance approximately 3,000 times compared to previous results, and confirmed secure key generation over 100 kilometers, surpassing the previous international record by more than two orders of magnitude.
- These outcomes mark pivotal milestones that move fiber-based quantum networks from theory toward practical implementation.
